title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen

progression.thoughts: Elizabeth Bennet’s character is sharp and witty.
Darcy’s letter completely shifts the perspective.

final_thoughts: A brilliant exploration of love and social class.

title: The Catcher in the Rye
author: J.D. Salinger

progression.thoughts: Holden’s voice is unique but a bit frustrating.

final_thoughts: A raw look into teenage alienation.

title: Brave New World
author: Aldous Huxley

progression.thoughts: The engineered society feels disturbingly plausible.

final_thoughts: A thought-provoking dystopia about control and happiness.

title: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring
author: J.R.R. Tolkien

progression.thoughts: The journey begins to feel epic as the fellowship forms.

final_thoughts: An incredible start to an epic fantasy saga.
